  "text": "The grafana/grafana codebase implements dashboard JSON model versioning and migration across multiple layers:\n\n## Core Migration Architecture\nThe migration system is orchestrated in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`, which defines a singleton `migrator` type that manages the migration pipeline. The main entry point is the `Migrate()` function, which applies frontend defaults, runs sequential schema version migrations (v2 through v42), and performs cleanup.     20→func Convert_V0_to_V1(in *dashv0.Dashboard, out *dashv1.Dashboard, scope conversion.Scope) error {
    21→	out.ObjectMeta = in.ObjectMeta
    22→
    23→	out.Spec.Object = in.Spec.Object
    24→
    25→	out.Status = dashv1.DashboardStatus{
    26→		Conversion: &dashv1.DashboardConversionStatus{
    27→			StoredVersion: ptr.To(dashv0.VERSION),
    28→		},
    29→	}
    30→
    31→	// the scope passed into this function is used in k8s apimachinery for migrations, but we also need the context
    32→	// to have what grafana expects in the request context, so that we can retrieve datasources for migrating
    33→	// some of the old dashboard schemas (these migrations used to be run in the frontend)
    34→	ctx := request.WithNamespace(context.Background(), in.GetNamespace())
    35→	nsInfo, err := types.ParseNamespace(in.GetNamespace())
    36→	if err != nil {
    37→		out.Status.Conversion.Failed = true
    38→		out.Status.Conversion.Error = ptr.To(err.Error())
    39→		return schemaversion.NewMigrationError(err.Error(), schemaversion.GetSchemaVersion(in.Spec.Object), schemaversion.LATEST_VERSION, "Convert_V0_to_V1")

    43→	// may not have access to all the datasources in the dashboard, but the migration still needs to take place
    44→	// in order to be able to convert between k8s versions (so that we have a guaranteed structure to convert between)
    45→	ctx, _ = identity.WithServiceIdentity(ctx, nsInfo.OrgID)

    47→	if err := migration.Migrate(ctx, out.Spec.Object, schemaversion.LATEST_VERSION); err != nil {

  "text": "The grafana/grafana codebase implements dashboard JSON model versioning and migration across multiple layers:\n\n## Core Migration Architecture\nThe migration system is orchestrated in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`, which defines a singleton `migrator` type that manages the migration pipeline. The main entry point is the `Migrate()` function, which applies frontend defaults, runs sequential schema version migrations (v2 through v42), and performs cleanup. The `Initialize()` function sets up the migrator with required dependencies like the DataSourceInfoProvider.\n\n## Schema Version Migration Functions\nIndividual version migration functions are defined in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/` directory:\n- `migrations.go` defines the `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` type and `GetMigrations()` function that registers all migrations (V2-V42)\n- Each version file (v2.go through v42.go) implements field-level migrations that upgrade specific dashboard JSON properties\n- Schema versions are tracked and validated using `GetSchemaVersion()` function\n- The system supports MIN_VERSION 0 and LATEST_VERSION 42\n\n## Field-Level Migration Utilities\nMigration utilities are provided in:\n- `migration_utils.go`: Contains type conversion helpers (GetStringValue, GetBoolValue, GetIntValue, GetFloatValue, ConvertToFloat, ConvertToInt)\n- `datasource_utils.go`: Provides datasource reference migration functions (MigrateDatasourceNameToRef, GetDataSourceRef, GetDefaultDSInstanceSettings)\n- These utilities support the field-level transformations needed across different schema versions\n\n## Frontend Defaults Application\n`frontend_defaults.go` implements the logic to apply DashboardModel and PanelModel constructor defaults, ensuring migrated dashboards match the frontend's expected structure. This includes applying defaults for panels, templating, annotations, and other dashboard properties.\n\n## Dashboard Schema Definitions\nDashboard schema is defined across multiple API versions:\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v0alpha1/`: Initial dashboard API version\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v1beta1/`: Beta version with enhanced structure\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v2alpha1/`: Alpha v2 with further refinements\n- `apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v2beta1/`: Current v2 beta structure\n\nEach version includes types.go, dashboard_spec.go, and constants.go defining the schema structure.\n\n## API Version Conversion\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/` handles conversions between Kubernetes API versions:\n- `conversion.go`: Registers conversion functions between v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, and v2beta1\n- `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`: Implement conversion logic between specific API versions\n- `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`: Handles the conversion from alpha to beta v2\n- Each conversion also applies the full schema version migration pipeline to ensure consistency\n\n## Error Handling and Metrics\n- `schemaversion/errors.go`: Defines migration-specific error types\n- `conversion/errors.go`: Defines conversion error types\n- `migration/metrics.go` and `conversion/metrics.go`: Track migration and conversion metrics\n\nThe system ensures backward compatibility by supporting migrations from schema version 0 to 42, with field-level transformations handling the evolution of dashboard structure, datasource references, panel configurations, and other key properties."

  "text": "The grafana/grafana codebase implements dashboard JSON model versioning and migration across multiple layers:\n\n## Core Migration Architecture\nThe migration system is orchestrated in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`, which defines a singleton `migrator` type that manages the migration pipeline. The main entry point is the `Migrate()` function, which applies frontend defaults, runs sequential schema version migrations (v2 through v42), and performs cleanup. The `Initialize()` function sets up the migrator with required dependencies like the DataSourceInfoProvider.\n\n## Schema Version Migration Functions\nIndividual version migration functions are defined in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/` directory:\n- `migrations.go` defines the `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` type and `GetMigrations()` function that registers all migrations (V2-V42)\n- Each version file (v2.go through v42.go) implements field-level migrations that upgrade specific dashboard JSON properties\n- Schema versions are tracked and validated using `GetSchemaVersion()` function\n- The system supports MIN_VERSION 0 and LATEST_VERSION 42\n\n## Field-Level Migration Utilities\nMigration utilities are provided in:\n- `migration_utils.go`: Contains type conversion helpers (GetStringValue, GetBoolValue, GetIntValue, GetFloatValue, ConvertToFloat, ConvertToInt)\n- `datasource_utils.go`: Provides datasource reference migration functions (MigrateDatasourceNameToRef, GetDataSourceRef, GetDefaultDSInstanceSettings)\n- These utilities support the field-level transformations needed across different schema versions\n\n## Frontend Defaults Application\n`frontend_defaults.go` implements the logic to apply DashboardModel and PanelModel constructor defaults, ensuring migrated dashboards match the frontend's expected structure. The main entry point is the `Migrate()` function, which applies frontend defaults, runs sequential schema version migrations (v2 through v42), and performs cleanup. The `Initialize()` function sets up the migrator with required dependencies like the DataSourceInfoProvider.

Individual version migration functions are defined in `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/` directory:

`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/` handles conversions between Kubernetes API versions:
- `conversion.go`: Registers conversion functions between v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, and v2beta1
- `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`: Implement conversion logic between specific API versions
- `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`: Handles the conversion from alpha to beta v2
- Each conversion also applies the full schema version migration pipeline to ensure consistency

- `schemaversion/errors.go`: Defines migration-specific error types
- `conversion/errors.go`: Defines conversion error types
- `migration/metrics.go` and `conversion/metrics.go`: Track migration and conversion metrics
